about.title: What is Tengri Yurt?
about.text: Tengri Yurt is a handcrafted architectural space rooted in the ancient nomadic traditions of Kazakhstan and reimagined for the modern world. Designed as a circular environment of connection and presence, it brings people together in nature — for retreats, eco-resorts, cultural projects, and transformational gatherings across the globe.

modal.title: A Brief History of the Yurt
modal.intro: The yurt (known as ger in Mongolian) is a portable, circular dwelling made of a flexible wooden lattice frame, covered with felt or skins. It has been the iconic home of nomadic peoples across Central Asia for over 3,000 years.
modal.origins_text: The word "yurt" comes from Turkic roots meaning "homeland," "dwelling," or "place of stay." The earliest evidence dates back to the Bronze Age and Scythian nomads around 600 BC. Turkic nomads popularized the trellis-walled design by the 6th century AD.
modal.history_text: Yurts were perfect for nomadic life — quick to assemble (about 1 hour), lightweight, wind-resistant, insulated against extreme temperatures. During the Mongol Empire, Genghis Khan used large yurts as mobile command centers.
modal.structure_text: Round for stability and airflow. Key parts: foldable lattice walls (kerege), roof poles (uni), central roof wheel (toono/shanyrak), felt layers for waterproofing and insulation.
modal.modern_text: Today yurts inspire glamping, eco-retreats, and modern adaptations worldwide. They symbolize nomadic heritage, sustainability, and connection to nature.
modal.conclusion: The yurt is one of humanity's oldest, smartest portable homes — born on the steppes, built to move with the people, and still thriving today.

questions.q: What does the word "yurt" mean in Turkic?
questions.explain: The word comes from Turkic roots meaning "homeland" or "place of stay."

questions.q: What is the Mongolian name for a yurt?
questions.explain: In Mongolia it is called "ger" (гэр), which simply means "home."

questions.q: How long does it take to assemble a traditional yurt?
questions.explain: Skilled nomads can put one up in about an hour — perfect for a mobile life.

questions.q: What is the central roof ring of a yurt called in Kazakh?
questions.explain: Shanyrak is the crown of the yurt and a symbol of the family and homeland in Kazakh culture.

questions.q: Which empire used large yurts as mobile command centers?
questions.explain: Genghis Khan and the Mongol Empire used yurts as mobile headquarters on the steppe.

questions.q: What are the lattice walls of a yurt called?
questions.explain: Kerege (or kana in Mongolian) are the foldable wooden lattice sections that form the wall.

questions.q: Roughly how many years have yurts been used in Central Asia?
questions.explain: Evidence of similar structures dates back to the Scythians and Bronze Age nomads.

questions.q: What natural material is traditionally used for yurt insulation?
questions.explain: Hand-pressed wool felt provides insulation and weather resistance.

yurtDescriptions.intimate: Traditional Kazakh 6-kanat yurt. 5×5 m, height 3.6 m. Ideal for private retreats and intimate gatherings of 15–20 people. Withstands temperatures from −40°C to +40°C.
yurtDescriptions.cozy: Traditional Kazakh 8-kanat yurt. 6×6 m, height 4 m. Perfect for family events, yoga sessions, creative workshops, and glamping with 25–30 guests.
yurtDescriptions.classic: Traditional Kazakh 12-kanat yurt. 8×8 m, height 4.7 m. Our most popular model for eco-resorts, cultural venues, and events hosting 35–55 people.
yurtDescriptions.spacious: Traditional Kazakh 16-kanat yurt. 9×9 m, height 4.8 m. The largest model — survived the strongest storm at Burning Man 2025, sheltering 5,000 guests.
yurtDescriptions.intimateHistory: The 6-kanat yurt is the classic compact form of the Kazakh nomadic dwelling. Built by hand using techniques passed down through generations, it offers the perfect balance of portability and comfort.
yurtDescriptions.cozyHistory: The 8-kanat yurt has long been the choice for extended families and small celebrations on the steppe. Its proportions balance warmth in winter and airflow in summer.
yurtDescriptions.classicHistory: The 12-kanat yurt is the classic format used for gatherings, ceremonies and hospitality across Central Asia.
yurtDescriptions.spaciousHistory: The 16-kanat model is the pinnacle of the traditional Kazakh yurt: a grand space for weddings, festivals and corporate events.

faq.title: Common Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before ordering

items.question: How much does shipping cost?
items.answer: Shipping is calculated based on destination and yurt size. Air freight (7-14 days) typically costs $2,000-$5,000. Sea freight (30-45 days) is $800-$2,500. We provide exact quotes after your inquiry.

items.question: Do you handle customs and import?
items.answer: We prepare all export documentation from Kazakhstan. Import duties vary by country — we'll advise on expected costs for your destination. Our team has shipped to 10+ countries successfully.

items.question: What's included in the installation?
items.answer: Professional installation is included. Our team of 1-3 specialists travels to your location. Setup takes 1-3 days depending on yurt size. You cover travel and accommodation costs.

items.question: What warranty do you offer?
items.answer: All yurts come with a 2-year warranty on structural components (frame, shanyrak, door). Felt and canvas coverings are warranted for 1 year. We provide maintenance guidance for long-term durability.

items.question: How long does production take?
items.answer: Production takes 30-60 days depending on model and customization. Each yurt is handcrafted by our team of 40+ artisans in Almaty, Kazakhstan. Rush orders may be available — ask us.

items.question: Can the yurt withstand extreme weather?
items.answer: Yes. Our yurts handle temperatures from -40°C to +40°C. The felt insulation naturally regulates temperature. The structure withstands winds up to 80+ km/h. We've installed in deserts, mountains, and tropical climates.

items.question: What are the payment terms?
items.answer: 50% deposit to begin production, 50% before shipping. We accept bank transfer and can provide a formal invoice. For orders over $50,000, custom payment schedules are available.

items.question: Can I customize the interior and exterior?
items.answer: Absolutely. Choose kerege color, cover material (white, silver, transparent), interior furnishings, and traditional accessories. Each yurt is made to your specifications.

aboutPage.heroTitle:
From the steppe
to every continent
aboutPage.heroSubtitle: Tengri Yurt was born in Almaty in 2010 with one belief: that the most sophisticated portable dwelling ever invented deserves a global audience. Fourteen years later, our yurts stand on every inhabited continent.
aboutPage.quote: The Kazakh yurt is the world's most sophisticated portable dwelling — three thousand years of engineering in felt and wood. Our job is to bring it to people who have never seen the steppe.

timeline.title: Founded in Almaty
timeline.body: Tengri Yurt is established by a team of craftspeople and entrepreneurs with a single goal: bring the traditional Kazakh yurt to the world without compromising its soul.

timeline.title: First export to Europe
timeline.body: A 6-kanat yurt is installed in Switzerland — the first Kazakh-made yurt in Central Europe. Word spreads through glamping and festival communities.

timeline.title: UNESCO heritage recognition
timeline.body: The art of Kazakh yurt-making is inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. Tengri Yurt becomes its commercial ambassador.

timeline.title: Yurt Hammam launch
timeline.body: We introduce the world's first traditional Kazakh yurt converted into a full spa: steam chamber, hot and cold plunge pools, cedar benches. A new category is born.

timeline.title: Miami Art Week
timeline.body: A Tengri yurt serves as a cultural pavilion at the Miami Art Fair. Over 3,000 visitors experience Kazakh interior craft. North America opens.

timeline.title: 42 countries, 6 continents
timeline.body: A 12-kanat yurt installed on New Zealand's South Island marks our 42nd country and our first in Oceania. Every inhabited continent reached.

materials.name: Tundyk — the crown
materials.desc: The circular opening at the apex of the yurt. A symbol of the sun, the universe, and the connection between earth and sky. Hand-bent from steam-treated wood.

materials.name: Kerege — the lattice
materials.desc: Folding willow lattice walls. A single kerege section can expand from 40 cm to 2.5 m. Woven without nails using traditional lashing techniques.

materials.name: Uyk — the roof poles
materials.desc: Curved wooden ribs connecting the crown to the walls. Their arc is calculated by eye — a skill that takes years to master.

materials.name: Koshma — the felt
materials.desc: Hand-rolled sheep's wool felt. Three layers insulate against −40°C winters and +40°C summers. No synthetic materials, no adhesives.

aboutPage.artisanP1: Every Tengri yurt is built by Kazakh master craftspeople — ustalar — who learned the trade from their parents, who learned from theirs. The knowledge is oral, tactile, and irreplaceable.
aboutPage.artisanP2: In 2018, UNESCO inscribed yurt-making on its Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. Tengri Yurt is the only company to commercialize this heritage at scale while keeping every step of production in Kazakhstan.
aboutPage.ctaEyebrow: Ready to own a piece of it?

contactPage.title: Let's talk yurts
contactPage.subtitle: Whether you're ordering a single yurt for your garden or sourcing twenty for a glamping resort — we want to hear from you. We typically reply within 24 hours.

faq.a: A standard yurt takes 30–60 days depending on the model. Custom requests may add 1–2 weeks.
Yes. We have delivered to 42 countries across all 6 inhabited continents. Shipping cost is calculated per order based on destination.
Our team installs within Kazakhstan and Central Asia. For international orders, we provide video guides and can connect you with local assembly partners.
Yes. We offer full customization: cover colour, felt patterns, furniture, lighting, heating. Describe your vision and we will advise.
Yes, short-term rental is available for events and festivals. Contact us with your dates and location.

hammamPage.heroSubtitle: Steam. Silence. Stone. The world's first traditional Kazakh yurt converted into a full spa experience — hot plunge, cold plunge, and steam chamber under an open sky.

features.title: Ancient ritual
features.body: Three thousand years of nomadic wellness wisdom. The steam ritual — banya on horseback — adapted for the modern world without losing its soul.

features.title: Contrast therapy
features.body: Hot steam followed by cold plunge. The contrast activates circulation, reduces inflammation, and grounds the nervous system.

features.title: Natural materials only
features.body: Cedar interior, hand-forged copper fixtures, felt insulation, kanat lattice walls. Nothing artificial, nothing out of place.

features.title: Anywhere on earth
features.body: Installed in 42 countries. Mountains, desert, lakeside, rooftop. The yurt hammam goes where a fixed spa cannot.

hammamPage.ritualP1: Kazakh nomads have used steam bathing since antiquity. The mobile steam tent — carried on horseback across the steppe — was both hygiene and ritual. Tengri Yurt is the first company to bring this ancient practice into a permanent, exportable form.
hammamPage.ritualP2: Every Yurt Hammam is built by the same ustalar — master artisans — who make our traditional yurts. The steam chamber, plunge pools, and kanat lattice walls are all hand-assembled on-site.

ritualSteps.title: Enter & breathe
ritualSteps.body: Take your place in the steam chamber. 80°C cedar air, the kanat filtering soft light.

ritualSteps.title: Heat cycle
ritualSteps.body: 15–20 minutes in dry heat. Sweat deeply. The felt walls hold warmth like no other material.

ritualSteps.title: Cold plunge
ritualSteps.body: Step into 10°C water. The shock is sharp and clean. Hold 1–3 minutes.

ritualSteps.title: Rest
ritualSteps.body: Return to the open air. Lie on the cedar bench. Let the sky do the rest.

hammamPage.ctaSubtitle: Each Yurt Hammam is built to order. Production takes 45–60 days, installation 3–4 days. Worldwide delivery included.
